key_insight: High-velocity air-fuel spraying can produce thick NiTi coatings that retain martensitic transformation and usable shape-memory behavior after annealing.
engineering_relevance: This offers a practical route to functional NiTi surface layers on steel components for wear-resistant, thermally active, or adaptive coatings.
method: NiTi coatings were deposited on mild steel by HVAF spray using four different parameter sets.
Coatings were characterized for composition, porosity, microstructure, phase transformation, and thermomechanical response.
Post-spray annealing was used to recover functional shape-memory behavior.
evidence_signal: The coated samples were 100–300 μm thick and were evaluated by microstructure/phase analysis plus bending, nanoindentation, and scratch tests, which showed thermal actuation and shape-memory effects.
watch_item: As-sprayed coatings are microstructurally inhomogeneous and defect-rich, so annealing and careful process control are needed to obtain reliable functional performance.
paper_title: HVAF Spraying of NiTi Coatings: Microstructure, Phase Transformation and Shape Memory Behavior
